---
title: "Market Performance & Concentration Dashboard"
format:
  html:
    theme: [cosmo, theme.scss]
    page-layout: full
    self-contained: false
---

<head>
  <meta name="description" content="Magnificent Seven vs S&P 500 Equal Weight and Market-Cap Weight Index comparison dashboard.">
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Outfit:wght@400;600&family=Inter:wght@400;500;600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
</head>

<body>

## Market Concentration: Magnificent Seven vs. Broad Market (Base: Jan 2020 = 100%)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-m7" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">M7 vs RSP</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Mega-Cap Tech Concentration vs. Equal-Weight S&P 500</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<span style="font-size: 13px; color: #8a90a6; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;">Comparing tech giants to the average stock</span>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-m7" onclick="toggleFullscreen()">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: m7-comparison-chart
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# Load M7 dataset
df = pd.read_csv("../downloads/m7_comparison.csv")
df['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['date'])

fig = go.Figure()

# Add M7 Index line (Vibrant Neon Green)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df['date'], y=df['m7_index'],
    mode='lines',
    line=dict(color='#00e676', width=2.5),
    name='Magnificent Seven Index (Equal Weight)'
))

# Add SPY line (Bright Blue)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df['date'], y=df['spy'],
    mode='lines',
    line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=2),
    name='S&P 500 ETF (SPY - Market-Cap Weighted)'
))

# Add RSP line (Vibrant Amber/Orange dashed)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df['date'], y=df['rsp'],
    mode='lines',
    line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5, dash='dash'),
    name='S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP - Average Stock)'
))

fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark',
    paper_bgcolor='#0c101b',  # Matching deep slate-blue background
    plot_bgcolor='#0c101b',
    autosize=True,
    dragmode='pan',
    margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            activecolor='#2962ff',
            font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11),
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(
        gridcolor='#202637',
        zerolinecolor='#202637',
        ticksuffix='%',
        title='Performance Index (Jan 2, 2020 = 100%)',
        fixedrange=True
    ),
    legend=dict(
        orientation='h',
        yanchor='bottom',
        y=1.02,
        xanchor='right',
        x=1,
        bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)'
    )
)

fig.update_xaxes(
    minallowed='2020-01-02',
    maxallowed='2026-07-05'
)

fig.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>
:::

<script>
function toggleFullscreen() {
  var el = document.getElementById('chart-container-m7');
  el.classList.toggle('fullscreen');
  var btn = document.getElementById('fullscreen-btn-m7');
  if (el.classList.contains('fullscreen')) {
    btn.innerHTML = "🗖 Exit Full Screen";
  } else {
    btn.innerHTML = "⛶ Full Screen";
  }
  // Let Plotly adjust to new size (after transition)
  setTimeout(function() {
    window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize'));
  }, 150);
}
</script>

</body>
